message:
refactor(libs): route every PATH/bin lookup through TARGET_HOME-aware preferred_bin_dir helpers + add gcloud SDK to candidate paths

Continues the TARGET_HOME-aware binary-resolution refactor from the
last few commits (e8fe3152, 5c735ede, 84a08c1f, 4fb2e7f2). Eight
script-lib files plus packages/onboard/onboard.sh previously hard-coded
the pattern:

    primary_bin_dir="${ACFS_BIN_DIR:-$base_home/.local/bin}"

That had two failure modes for non-default installs:

  1. Ignored the install-state-recorded `bin_dir` for installs where
     ACFS_BIN_DIR wasn't exported in the calling shell — so doctor, info,
     status, stack, onboard, smoke_test all could miss binaries living
     in a non-standard target directory.

  2. Leaked caller (often root/sudo) state into target-user repairs:
     autofix_version_managers checked NVM_DIR/PYENV_ROOT from the
     caller's environment without first verifying the directory was
     actually under the resolved runtime home.

This commit threads every lookup through one of:

  - info.sh:  info_preferred_bin_dir()
  - status.sh: _status_preferred_bin_dir()
  - stack.sh: _stack_target_bin_dir()
  - smoke_test.sh: _smoke_resolve_bootstrap_state_file() + bin_dir helpers
  - onboard.sh: onboard_preferred_bin_dir()
  - doctor.sh: doctor-side resolution that consults installed/system
    state.json before ambient $HOME/.acfs state
  - doctor_fix.sh: doctor_fix_runtime_acfs_home() now honors TARGET_HOME
    over a stray ACFS_HOME, plus a new doctor_fix_runtime_bin_dir()
  - autofix_version_managers.sh: autofix_version_managers_runtime_home()
    + autofix_version_managers_path_belongs_to_runtime_home() guard

Each helper applies the same precedence:
  state.json bin_dir  >  ACFS_BIN_DIR env  >  $HOME/.local/bin fallback

with sanitization (`*_sanitize_abs_nonroot_path`) to reject empty,
relative, or `/` paths from any source.

Also extends the search-path list in info.sh, status.sh, and doctor.sh
to include `$base_home/google-cloud-sdk/bin` (gcloud installs there by
default and was previously invisible to ACFS bin lookups), and now
filters every candidate dir through `[[ -d "$dir" ]] || continue` to
avoid polluting PATH with non-existent entries.

zoxide-specific fix in update.sh / update_repair_zoxide_install:
when ACFS_BIN_DIR differs from `$HOME/.local/bin`, the upstream zoxide
installer drops its binary in `$HOME/.local/bin`, but a stale shim
under $ACFS_BIN_DIR (which comes earlier in PATH) can keep resolving
to the old version. The repair now symlinks `$ACFS_BIN_DIR/zoxide` to
the freshly installed `$HOME/.local/bin/zoxide` so future shells pick
up the upgrade.

New / updated test coverage:

  - test_autofix_version_managers.sh
      test_nvm_check_prefers_target_home_over_poisoned_home: simulates a
      caller HOME with NVM_DIR/.nvm pointing at the wrong tree and
      verifies TARGET_HOME wins.

  - tests/unit/lib/test_update.bats
      "update_repair_zoxide_install: normalizes custom shim..." asserts
      the new symlink-to-canonical behavior, plus updated greps for
      the new info_preferred_bin_dir / _status_preferred_bin_dir hooks.

  - tests/unit/test_doctor_fix.sh: covers doctor_fix_runtime_bin_dir
    and the new acfs_home precedence.

  - tests/unit/test_changelog_export_status.sh (NEW, ~380 LOC):
    targeted regression suite for changelog.sh, export-config.sh,
    support.sh, cheatsheet.sh, dashboard.sh, continue.sh, state.sh,
    notify.sh, webhook.sh, notifications.sh, autofix.sh,
    autofix_existing.sh, plus the libs touched here. Verifies that
    output formatters, state writers, and notification helpers all
    behave correctly under both ambient-HOME and TARGET_HOME resolution
    modes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -263,14 +263,45 @@ onboard_resolve_runtime_home() {
 
 ONBOARD_RUNTIME_HOME="$(onboard_resolve_runtime_home)"
 
+onboard_preferred_bin_dir() {
+    local runtime_home="${1:-${ONBOARD_RUNTIME_HOME:-${_ONBOARD_CURRENT_HOME:-/root}}}"
+    local candidate=""
+
+    candidate="$(onboard_sanitize_abs_nonroot_path "$(onboard_read_state_string "$ACFS_HOME/state.json" "bin_dir" 2>/dev/null || true)" 2>/dev/null || true)"
+    if [[ -n "$candidate" ]]; then
+        printf '%s\n' "$candidate"
+        return 0
+    fi
+
+    candidate="$(onboard_sanitize_abs_nonroot_path "$(onboard_read_state_string "$_ONBOARD_SYSTEM_STATE_FILE" "bin_dir" 2>/dev/null || true)" 2>/dev/null || true)"
+    if [[ -n "$candidate" ]]; then
+        printf '%s\n' "$candidate"
+        return 0
+    fi
+
+    candidate="$(onboard_sanitize_abs_nonroot_path "${ACFS_BIN_DIR:-}" 2>/dev/null || true)"
+    if [[ -n "$candidate" ]]; then
+        printf '%s\n' "$candidate"
+        return 0
+    fi
+
+    printf '%s\n' "$runtime_home/.local/bin"
+}
+
 onboard_runtime_binary_path() {
     local name="${1:-}"
     local runtime_home="${ONBOARD_RUNTIME_HOME:-${_ONBOARD_CURRENT_HOME:-/root}}"
-    local primary_bin_dir="${ACFS_BIN_DIR:-$runtime_home/.local/bin}"
+    local primary_bin_dir=""
     local candidate=""
+    local path_dir=""
+    local sanitized_dir=""
+    local -a path_entries=()
 
     [[ -n "$name" ]] || return 1
 
+    primary_bin_dir="$(onboard_preferred_bin_dir "$runtime_home" 2>/dev/null || true)"
+    [[ -n "$primary_bin_dir" ]] || primary_bin_dir="$runtime_home/.local/bin"
+
     for candidate in \
         "$primary_bin_dir/$name" \
         "$runtime_home/.local/bin/$name" \
@@ -279,6 +310,7 @@ onboard_runtime_binary_path() {
         "$runtime_home/.bun/bin/$name" \
         "$runtime_home/.atuin/bin/$name" \
         "$runtime_home/go/bin/$name" \
+        "$runtime_home/google-cloud-sdk/bin/$name" \
         "/usr/local/go/bin/$name" \
         "/usr/local/bin/$name" \
         "/usr/bin/$name" \
@@ -290,10 +322,20 @@ onboard_runtime_binary_path() {
         fi
     done
 
-    if command -v "$name" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
-        command -v "$name"
-        return 0
-    fi
+    IFS=':' read -r -a path_entries <<< "${PATH:-}"
+    for path_dir in "${path_entries[@]}"; do
+        sanitized_dir="$(onboard_sanitize_abs_nonroot_path "$path_dir" 2>/dev/null || true)"
+        [[ -n "$sanitized_dir" ]] || continue
+        case "$sanitized_dir" in
+            "$runtime_home"|"$runtime_home"/*)
+                candidate="$sanitized_dir/$name"
+                if [[ -x "$candidate" ]]; then
+                    printf '%s\n' "$candidate"
+                    return 0
+                fi
+                ;;
+        esac
+    done
 
     return 1
 }

+autofix_version_managers_runtime_home() {
+    local runtime_home=""
+
+    runtime_home="$(autofix_sanitize_abs_nonroot_path "${TARGET_HOME:-}" 2>/dev/null || true)"
+    if [[ -n "$runtime_home" ]]; then
+        printf '%s\n' "$runtime_home"
+        return 0
+    fi
+
+    runtime_home="$(autofix_sanitize_abs_nonroot_path "${HOME:-}" 2>/dev/null || true)"
+    if [[ -n "$runtime_home" ]]; then
+        printf '%s\n' "$runtime_home"
+        return 0
+    fi
+
+    return 1
+}
+
+# Only trust env-provided directories when they belong to the resolved
+# runtime home, otherwise root/sudo callers can leak their own XDG/NVM/PYENV
+# paths into target-user repairs.
+autofix_version_managers_path_belongs_to_runtime_home() {
+    local candidate_path=""
+    local runtime_home=""
+
+    candidate_path="$(autofix_sanitize_abs_nonroot_path "${1:-}" 2>/dev/null || true)"
+    runtime_home="$(autofix_sanitize_abs_nonroot_path "${2:-}" 2>/dev/null || true)"
+
+    [[ -n "$candidate_path" ]] || return 1
+    [[ -n "$runtime_home" ]] || return 1
+    [[ "$candidate_path" == "$runtime_home" || "$candidate_path" == "$runtime_home"/* ]]
+}
+
 autofix_version_managers_restore() {
     local restore_command="${1:-}"

+    fi

+    rm -rf "$temp_root"
+    return 0
